mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly payroll growth above 150,000 jobs signals robust labor market momentum that increases consumer confidence and disposable income, driving equity valuations higher over the subsequent 20 trading days through elevated earnings expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:March 2014 payroll level at 137,928k suggests ongoing recovery trajectory
failure_condition: Monthly payroll growth exceeds 150k in > 70% of observations yet 20-day forward returns remain at or below baseline (median return < 0.5%), or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate remains near zero (below 0.25%) while inflation measured by CPIAUCSL stays below 2.5%, indicating monetary accommodation that suppresses real discount rates and sustains equity risk appetite over a 60-day forward horizon.
regime_hint: zero lower bound monetary accommodation with subdued inflation
failure_condition: Trigger condition met in > 60% of sample yet 60-day forward returns are not significantly positive (median < 0.8%), or the interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts measured by HOUST exceed 900,000 annualized units, signaling strong residential construction demand that multiplies through the economy via employment and consumer spending, producing positive equity returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:February 2014 HOUST at 907,000 units, near trigger threshold
failure_condition: HOUST exceeds 900k in > 50% of observations yet 20-day forward returns show no significant positive bias (t-statistic < 1.5), or Rank IC < 0.025 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production measured by INDPRO declines month-over-month by more than 0.5%, signaling demand weakness or supply-side constraints that compress profit margins and reduce equity valuations over the subsequent 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:February 2014 INDPRO at 101.6276, showing recent volatility
failure_condition: Month-over-month INDPRO decline exceeds -0.5% in > 40% of triggered instances yet 5-day forward returns remain positive in > 55% of cases, or Rank IC > -0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales measured by RSAFS and real GDP growth measured by GDPC1 both accelerate simultaneously, indicating broad-based demand expansion that supports equity earnings growth and valuations over a 60-day horizon through multiplier effects.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month growth > 0.8% AND GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth > 2.5%
evidence_refs: fact:March 2014 RSAFS at 433,907M, Q3 2013 GDPC1 at 15,942.3B
regime_hint: synchronized demand acceleration in late-cycle expansion
failure_condition: Both trigger conditions met in > 50% of observations yet 60-day forward returns show no significant positive skew (median < 0.5%), or the interaction coefficient is not significant (p > 0.15) in a two-factor model.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate measured by UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window, signaling labor market deterioration that increases recession risk and triggers equity risk-off behavior, producing negative 20-day forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:March 2014 UNRATE at 6.7%, down from prior year levels indicating recovery
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession onset
failure_condition: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in 3-month window in > 35% of sample yet 20-day forward returns remain positive in > 60% of triggered cases, or Rank IC > -0.015.

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate rises above 0.15% while year-over-year inflation measured by CPIAUCSL exceeds 2.0%, signaling monetary tightening in response to inflation that increases equity discount rates and reduces valuations over a 20-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:March 2014 FEDFUNDS at 0.08%, CPIAUCSL at 235.64 with modest inflation
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with rising inflation
failure_condition: Both trigger conditions met in > 40% of observations yet 20-day forward returns are not significantly negative (median > 0.3%), or the interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.12).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production measured by INDPRO accelerates with a 3-month growth rate exceeding 1.2% annualized, signaling robust supply-side capacity utilization and productivity gains that support profit margins and equity valuations over a 60-day forward horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:February 2014 INDPRO at 101.6276, reflecting post-recession manufacturing recovery
regime_hint: supply-side expansion and capacity utilization recovery
failure_condition: INDPRO 3-month annualized growth exceeds 1.2% in > 55% of observations yet 60-day forward returns show no significant positive bias (t-statistic < 1.3), or Rank IC < 0.025 over any 2-year rolling window.
